question: What's the best time to visit Rockhampton QLD with kids?
answer: April to October is absolutely brilliant for visiting Rocky with the little ones! You'll get gorgeous sunny days without the sticky summer humidity. The winter months (June-August) are particularly beaut - perfect for exploring outdoors without overheating. Just avoid January-February when it's scorching hot and wet. School holidays book out fast, so get in early if you're planning a trip during those times, mate!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Rockhampton QLD?
answer: Good news - several holiday parks around Rocky welcome your furry family members! Many parks have designated pet-friendly cabins or powered sites where dogs are allowed. You'll usually need to keep them on a leash and clean up after them. It's always worth ringing ahead to check their specific pet policies and any additional fees. Some parks have restrictions on breeds or sizes, so best to double-check before booking.

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Rockhampton QLD during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay around $120-$200 per night for a family cabin sleeping four to six people. Powered caravan sites are more budget-friendly at $45-$70 per night. Prices jump about 20-30% during peak times like Easter and Christmas holidays. Booking a few months ahead can snag you better rates, and midweek stays are often cheaper than weekends. Many parks offer discounts for longer stays too!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Pack plenty of sunscreen, hats, and swimmers - the kids will be in and out of the pool all day! Bring insect repellent, basic first aid supplies, and any comfort items like favourite teddies. Most cabins have kitchens, so BYO pantry staples. Don't forget outdoor toys, bikes or scooters, and torches for night-time adventures. A portable fan is handy in summer, and always pack more clothes than you think you'll need!

question: What is there to do near Rockhampton QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: You're spoilt for choice! The Rockhampton Zoo is fantastic and free - kids absolutely love it. Check out the Botanic Gardens, Kershaw Gardens, or head to nearby beaches at Yeppoon (30 mins away). Capricorn Caves are ace for adventurous families, and the heritage village showcases local history. Many holiday parks have their own pools, playgrounds, and jumping pillows to keep the littlies entertained without leaving the park!
